creator: comet@transbay.net
text:
This bug occurs in the 7.0-RELEASE live file system CD too as well as the 6.2-RELEASE.

The /usr/bin/calendar program reports August 3rd as the anniversary of the
"Massacre of the Pidjiguiti in Buinea-bissau"
but it should be spelled as:
"Massacre of the Pidjiguiti in Guinea-Bissau"

This seminal 1959 event in which 50 striking dock workers were killed eventually resulted in the independence of Guinea-Bissau from Portugal.

http://wapedia.mobi/en/Pijiguiti_Massacre

Fix: 

The contents of the /usr/share/calendar/calendar.holiday file need to be changed:
Change the line:
08/03   Massacre of the Pidjiguiti in Buinea-bissau
to:
08/03   Massacre of the Pidjiguiti in Guinea-Bissau
How-To-Repeat: /usr/bin/calendar -t03.08
